key_insight: ABOPD uses on-policy distillation to keep antibody CDR-H3 generation aligned with native geometry during recursive denoising, improving structural fidelity.
clinical_relevance: By better preserving antigen-facing loop shape, the method could help generate more accurate antibody candidates for therapeutic discovery workflows.
method: Trains with privileged native geometry to supervise the model on states it reaches during its own denoising trajectory.
Targets the mismatch between standard denoising training and recursive generation for flexible CDR loops.
Focuses on CDR-H3 antibody design, where backbone drift can accumulate and hurt loop geometry.
evidence_signal: In RAbD CDR-H3 evaluation, ABOPD reduced RMSD from 2.37 Å to 1.95 Å, outperforming supervised fine-tuning and offline distillation controls.
watch_item: Results are reported on a structural recovery benchmark, so broader generalization to diverse antibodies, antigens, and wet-lab binding outcomes still needs validation.
paper_title: ABOPD: Antibody CDR Design via On-Policy Distillation
